prompts.title: Inbound Receiving SOP
prompts.prompt: Act as a warehouse manager at a Bhiwandi DC (Indian e-commerce fulfilment, 24K orders/day). Write a complete inbound-receiving SOP. Cover: (1) appointment scheduling, (2) dock allocation, (3) seal + GPS verification, (4) quantity + quality check, (5) GRN entry in WMS, (6) discrepancy flagging (overage, shortage, damage), (7) put-away strategy (random / dedicated), (8) FIFO labelling, (9) supplier scorecard input, (10) photo + e-signature. Constraints: align with WMS (Manhattan / Increff / Unicommerce); 4-min target per pallet; comply with E-Way bill + GST; 3-bid camera footage retained for 30 days.

prompts.title: KPI Dashboard for DC Manager
prompts.prompt: Act as a DC operations head. Build a daily KPI dashboard for warehouse leadership covering: (1) inbound (lines received, dock-to-stock time), (2) inventory (accuracy %, days on hand, ageing), (3) outbound (units picked / packed / shipped, on-time-shipment %), (4) productivity (units per labour hour by zone), (5) quality (pick error rate, mis-ship rate), (6) safety (incidents, near-miss, days since last LTI), (7) cost (₹ per order, overtime hours), (8) NPS (customer + driver). Constraints: refresh daily 9 AM IST; mobile-friendly; alerts on threshold breach; integrated with WMS + TMS.

prompts.title: Safety Procedures for Forklift Operations
prompts.prompt: Act as a warehouse-safety officer. Write a forklift operations safety procedure for a 60K sqft warehouse with 8 forklifts (BS-VI diesel + electric mix). Cover: (1) operator certification (state-certified license + in-house training), (2) pre-operation checklist, (3) load handling rules, (4) speed limits, (5) pedestrian segregation (yellow lines + horn protocol), (6) charging area safety (battery acid, ventilation), (7) incident reporting, (8) PPE, (9) shift handover. Constraints: align with Factories Act + IS 4660 for forklift; include Hindi safety signage; toolbox talk weekly; near-miss zero-tolerance.

prompts.title: Continuous Improvement Kaizen Framework
prompts.prompt: Act as a Lean black belt. Implement a kaizen + continuous improvement program at a 70K sqft DC. Cover: (1) value-stream mapping (current vs future state), (2) 7 waste identification (TIMWOOD), (3) 5S implementation, (4) employee suggestion scheme (₹500 micro-rewards + ₹50K annual top), (5) weekly kaizen blitz (1 problem, 5 days, cross-functional team), (6) measurement (cycle-time, throughput, defect rate), (7) DMAIC for major improvement projects, (8) Gemba walk discipline, (9) annual kaizen summit. Constraints: respect Indian shop-floor culture; multilingual (Hindi/regional); ROI track per kaizen project; align with TPS principles.

prompts.title: Staff Scheduling for Peak Season
prompts.prompt: Act as a workforce planning manager. Build staff schedule for Big Billion Days week at a Bengaluru DC (peak 32K orders/day vs daily 8K). Cover: (1) headcount projection (3 shifts, additional 180 contract), (2) cross-training plan (pickers + packers + sorters), (3) overtime + premium-rate matrix, (4) meal + rest break compliance (Shops Act 9 hrs / 48 wk), (5) attendance tracking (biometric + RFID), (6) absenteeism buffer 12%, (7) DEI + female-friendly shift, (8) housing for migrant labour. Constraints: align with Maharashtra/Karnataka labour code; PF + ESI for contract; INR cost per order during peak.

prompts.title: Pick Path Optimization for Pickers
prompts.prompt: Act as a WMS engineer. Optimise picker path in a 40K sqft DC with 12 zones, 1,800 SKUs. Cover: (1) batch-picking strategy (8 orders/batch), (2) zone-based pick (start at A finish at L), (3) S-shape vs Largest-Gap heuristic, (4) hot-SKU re-slotting (top 200 SKUs near packing), (5) pick density per aisle, (6) RF-gun + voice-pick comparison, (7) error-reduction (double-scan), (8) pick rate target 180 lines/hour. Constraints: integrate with Unicommerce; SKU velocity refreshed weekly; ergonomics — pick height 0.4-1.6m sweet spot.

prompts.title: Returns Processing SOP
prompts.prompt: Act as a returns operations manager. Build returns-processing SOP for a Tier-1 D2C beauty brand (12% return rate, 1,200 returns/day). Cover: (1) return triggers (customer-initiated, RTO, refused-delivery), (2) inbound + grading (A — resaleable, B — refurbish, C — scrap), (3) refurbishment SOP (clean, repack, relabel), (4) refund / exchange processing (T+3 SLA), (5) reverse-logistics partner (Shiprocket Returns), (6) DPDP-compliant data deletion, (7) loss-prevention (camera + audit), (8) sustainability (recycling, donation tie-up). Constraints: align with consumer protection norms; INR cost-of-returns per piece; minimise B + C grade leakage; RTO root-cause feedback to upstream.

prompts.title: Cycle Count + Inventory Audit
prompts.prompt: Act as an internal auditor at a manufacturing warehouse. Design a cycle-count + annual inventory audit program for a ₹95 Cr inventory. Cover: (1) ABC-based count frequency (A monthly, B quarterly, C annually), (2) random + scheduled count mix, (3) reconciliation rules (zero tolerance vs 1% threshold), (4) discrepancy investigation (root cause), (5) write-off authority matrix, (6) annual physical (December close), (7) external auditor coordination, (8) IFC + Internal Financial Control sign-off, (9) GST-compliant adjustments. Constraints: align with Companies Act IFC; tally / SAP entries audit-ready; protect against shrinkage; cycle count during off-peak.

prompts.title: Cross-Dock Logistics Design
prompts.prompt: Act as a logistics network designer. Design a cross-dock facility at JNPT, Mumbai for an Indian retail chain (40 stores, daily replenishment from 8 inbound suppliers). Cover: (1) flow design (inbound to outbound in 6 hours), (2) dock schedule (truck appointment in 30-min slots), (3) staging area, (4) sortation (manual + chute), (5) outbound truck building (route-based), (6) WMS-light deployment, (7) labour + MHE, (8) KPI (throughput, dwell time, cost per pallet), (9) capex ₹4 Cr. Constraints: align with port authority; INR cost saving vs traditional DC; FIFO compliance; weekly review.

prompts.title: Cold-Storage Operations SOP
prompts.prompt: Act as a cold-storage operations manager (think Snowman Logistics scale). Build SOPs for a 20K MT capacity cold-storage operating 3 zones (-25°C, 0-4°C, 12-15°C). Cover: (1) chamber temperature monitoring (continuous via IoT), (2) entry-exit door management (rapid-roll), (3) energy management (compressor schedule, off-peak), (4) staff PPE (insulated wear, max 2-hr exposure / shift), (5) product handling FIFO, (6) excursion management (if temp drift > 2°C for > 30 min — quarantine), (7) regulatory (FSSAI for food, CDSCO if pharma), (8) preventive maintenance (compressors, evaporators, condensers). Constraints: align with WHO PQS for vaccine cold-chain; INR power-cost monitoring; backup gen-set + UPS.

prompts.title: Damage Investigation Report
prompts.prompt: Act as a quality + claims manager. A truck arrived at our DC with 18% of cartons damaged (water + impact). Write a 600-word damage investigation report covering: (1) shipment details (origin, route, transporter, e-way bill), (2) on-arrival inspection findings (with photos), (3) root cause hypothesis (loading, transit, weather), (4) liability assignment (transporter / supplier / packaging / DC), (5) financial impact (₹5.6L), (6) insurance claim filing (Marine + Inland transit), (7) corrective action (packaging upgrade, transporter SLA review), (8) preventive plan. Constraints: align with insurance documentation; INR claim with proof; cite consignment note + bilty; 7-day claim filing.

prompts.title: Hazmat Storage Compliance Audit
prompts.prompt: Act as a hazmat warehouse auditor. Audit a 15K sqft chemical storage warehouse in Ankleshwar, Gujarat. Cover: (1) PESO + Factories Act compliance, (2) MSIHC Rules categorisation, (3) chemical compatibility matrix + segregation, (4) fire-suppression (foam + sprinkler + dry chem), (5) bunding + spill containment, (6) ventilation + LEV, (7) MSDS availability + training, (8) emergency response (offsite + onsite plan), (9) DGFASLI inspections + records. Constraints: align with Factories Act Cl. 41 + MSIHC 1989 (amended); cite recent fire incidents; vendor responsibility on hazmat transport; documented hazop.

prompts.title: WMS Implementation Project Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a WMS implementation lead. Build a 9-month plan to implement Manhattan WMOS at a ₹140 Cr DC operation. Phases: (1) discovery (current state, design, gap analysis — month 1-2), (2) configuration + integration (ERP, TMS, e-com, accounting — month 3-4), (3) data migration (master + transactional — month 4-5), (4) testing (unit, integration, UAT — month 5-6), (5) training (DC team, IT, business — month 6-7), (6) go-live (parallel run 14 days — month 7), (7) hyper-care + stabilisation (month 8-9). Cover: budget ₹4.2 Cr; team; risk register; KPIs + payback. Constraints: protect peak operations; backup plan; vendor accountability.

prompts.title: Energy Audit + Power Reduction
prompts.prompt: Act as an energy auditor (BEE-empanelled). Audit a 90K sqft DC consuming 12 lakh kWh/year (₹1.4 Cr). Build a reduction plan to cut 30%. Cover: (1) baseline (lighting, HVAC, MHE charging, conveyors, office), (2) lighting upgrade (LED + motion sensor), (3) HVAC efficiency (BEE 5-star, VFD), (4) skylights + daylight harvesting, (5) rooftop solar (200 kWp), (6) MHE conversion to lithium battery, (7) sub-metering + monitoring (EMS), (8) staff awareness, (9) ROI per intervention. Constraints: align with PAT scheme + ECBC; INR investment ₹95L; payback < 4 years.

prompts.title: Driver / Trucker Welfare Program
prompts.prompt: Act as a fleet HR manager at an Indian logistics company (3,000 drivers). Design a driver welfare + retention program. Cover: (1) compensation benchmarking (per km + per trip + bonus), (2) PF + ESI + accident insurance ₹15L, (3) family-medical scheme, (4) on-route facilities (washrooms, medical, chai stops, secure parking), (5) driving-hour discipline (Motor Vehicles Act compliance), (6) skill upgrade (defensive driving, fuel-efficient driving, GPS), (7) family engagement (school scholarships, festival hampers), (8) GPS-monitored safe-driving incentive, (9) grievance redress + helpline. Constraints: respect rural-migrant background; bilingual training; ESG outcome tracking; align with Code on Wages 2019.
